title: Air-fryer cod fillet
subtitle: Flaky white fish cooked in 12 minutes
excerpt: Cod fillets brushed with olive oil, seasoned with salt and pepper, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes. Cod requires no pre-treatment and no flipping. The air fryer produces a lightly golden top with moist, flaking flesh inside.
sourceNotes: Cod is the most historically significant fish in British cooking and the basis of fish and chips. Fresh cod fillets have a mild flavour and large, firm flakes that hold well in the air fryer. The main risk with cod is overcooking, which turns the flakes rubbery. The air fryer cooks cod evenly without the uneven browning or moisture loss of a dry pan.

content.text: Rest the fillet for 1 minute after cooking. Cod continues cooking from residual heat after the basket is removed, and the flakes will separate cleanly after a brief rest rather than tearing when lifted immediately. The rest also gives the juices time to redistribute so the fillet does not release a pool of liquid onto the plate.

items.prepNote: skin-on or skinless, about 150 to 180g each

content.text: Pat the {{cod-fillet}} fillets dry with kitchen paper. Brush all over with {{olive-oil}} and season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Place the fillets in the basket, skin-side down if the skin is on. Cook at 200°C for 10 minutes for thinner fillets (under 2.5 cm) or 12 minutes for thicker pieces. The fish is done when it flakes easily at the thickest part and the flesh is fully opaque. Rest for 1 minute before serving. Squeeze over {{lemon}} at the table.

items.symptom: Cod fillet crumbles when lifted from the basket
items.fix: Very thin tail-end pieces cook in 8 minutes. A well-cooked cod fillet should come apart into distinct flakes, not crumble. If it is crumbling, it has been overcooked; check earlier on the next attempt

items.cause: Oil not applied, or basket not preheated
items.fix: Brush or spray the basket lightly before adding the fish. Preheating ensures the basket is hot when the fish goes in, which helps it release cleanly

items.cause: Fish still very cold from the fridge when placed in the air fryer
items.fix: Remove from the fridge 10 minutes before cooking. Very cold fish needs longer to cook through, leading to overcooked edges before the centre is done

content.text: Air-fryer cod is a quick midweek fish dinner. Serve with chips, peas, and tartare sauce for a deconstructed fish and chips, alongside rice and steamed greens, or flaked over a tomato salad with capers and red onion. The 12-minute cook time is the fastest route to a properly cooked cod fillet without using a frying pan.
